We're waiting anyway.<|17.70|> <|17.70|> Okay.<|19.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It has been two weeks and I am still in awe of his gracious and hospitable gesture.<|10.88|> <|12.88|> Awe, wonder, epiphany, and the most unexpected moment.<|20.64|> <|20.64|> Amen.<|21.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.92|> When I wanted to rush home to comfortable clothes and an evening of television, a stranger whose beautiful relationship with his son interrupted my self-preoccupation and reintroduced me to the vastness of God's good and mysterious world.<|19.10|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> world, prompting me to pay attention long enough to choose the unfamiliar path at the<|7.00|> <|7.00|> prompting of someone I'd never before seen and may never see again.<|15.00|> <|15.00|> When attended to and cultivated, awe is as natural for Christians, for human beings of<|24.00|> <|24.00|> all stripes, as taking our<|27.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> next breath.
